name: Australian Poached Rabbit with Bruschetta
recipeIngredient: 2 ½ cups extra virgin olive oil
1 crusty Italian ring loaf of bread (preferably day-old)
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 6 liters of salted water to a boil in a large saucepan.
2. Add onion, celery, carrot, herbs, and peppercorns and return to the boil. Skim any surface foam.
3. Add rabbit and return to the boil. Reduce heat to the lowest setting, cover, and gently simmer for 1 hour, or until rabbit is tender. Remove from heat and cool rabbit in cooking liquid.
4. Remove rabbit from the liquid, strip meat from bones, and place it in a single layer on a plate or tray. Season meat generously with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
5. Return bones to the cooking liquid and boil again to make stock for freezing, if desired.
6. Transfer rabbit to a bowl. Add garlic, sage, and oil just to cover. Using your hands, mix ingredients to combine, then cover and refrigerate for 48 hours.
7. Cut bread into 1cm-thick slices and brush both sides with the remaining oil combined with 1 teaspoon sea salt. Place in a single layer on a rack on an oven tray and bake at 150°C for 30 minutes, or until pale golden. Using a slotted spoon, remove rabbit from the oil and serve at room temperature with warm bruschetta, mesclun, and lemon wedges, if desired.
